method: Static, line-grounded audit of each pinned corpus package after the current Pigo runtime shims. Each declared extension entrypoint and the code behind its primary workflow were inspected; current load/registration artifacts were used only to identify reached paths.
caveat: These verdicts do not claim workflow execution. likely_compatible means no remaining Pigo-specific blocker was found statically and still requires the stated smoke; partial means a useful subset appears viable; main_feature_blocked means the package loads but its defining workflow cannot run; load_blocked means at least one declared entrypoint cannot load.

entries.explanation: The inspected workflows use FileHandle, streaming TextDecoder, AbortSignal reasons, finite child processes, createAgentSession, and createWriteStream, all covered by current Pigo shims; no native addon, socket, worker, WASM, or DOM dependency was found.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Run real knowledge-base, web-tool, scheduler, and child-session smokes before claiming proven workflow compatibility.

entries.explanation: The MCP stdio client imports and constructs PassThrough streams, so a name-only stream stub would load falsely without providing the required duplex transport.
entries.remediation: Implement stream-capable child stdio and MCP transport semantics, or provide an intentional native Go MCP adapter.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and connect it to a real stdio MCP server.

entries.explanation: Search, fetch, extraction, and SSRF IP checks are statically viable, but optional Chrome-cookie import requires PBKDF2 and AES decipher APIs that Pigo does not provide.
entries.remediation: Add standards-faithful PBKDF2 and AES decipher support only if Chrome-cookie import is prioritized.
entries.conditions: Smoke ordinary search/fetch/extraction separately from authenticated Chrome-cookie import.

entries.explanation: Basic finite foreground and background subagents appear viable, while rich UI, watchdog tool factories, and LSP diagnostics depend on missing keyText, Markdown, createReadOnlyTools, and writable long-lived child stdio.
entries.remediation: Validate the core subagent path first; treat rich TUI, tool factories, and interactive process pipes as a separate compatibility project.
entries.conditions: Classify basic foreground/background execution independently from watchdog and rich-UI workflows.

entries.explanation: The mandatory persistence backend loads better-sqlite3 after createRequire setup, so the extension cannot initialize in Pigo's pure-Go runtime.
entries.remediation: Use an extension-supported non-native backend or design an explicit Go or service-backed SQLite integration; do not stub better-sqlite3.
entries.conditions: Load the extension, migrate its database, and exercise persistence and FTS.

entries.explanation: Core initialization eagerly requires createCodingTools, createReadOnlyTools, SettingsManager, SettingsList, and Text rather than touching one optional symbol lazily.
entries.remediation: Implement the faithful tool-factory, settings, and TUI surfaces as an explicit compatibility project.
entries.conditions: Load the extension, launch an agent, and open its settings UI.

entries.explanation: The inspected paths use ordinary fetch and command registration; the only expected prerequisite is the configured Remnic daemon and token.
entries.remediation: None in Pigo.
entries.conditions: Run the commands against a real authenticated Remnic daemon.

entries.explanation: The mandatory analysis backend resolves an @ast-grep N-API .node binary, which Sobek cannot execute.
entries.remediation: Use an upstream executable, WASM, or pure-JavaScript backend, or undertake native-addon support as a separate design project.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and execute lens map and analysis workflows.

entries.explanation: Pigo's CommonJS bundling resolves the deferred @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk dynamic import at compile time even when that provider path is not selected.
entries.remediation: Correct dynamic-import externalization and package resolution, then install and test the real SDK rather than faking its exports.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and run its AI-provider workflow.

entries.explanation: A real vm context and Script are central to workflow execution, and agent setup also requires missing createCodingTools and SettingsManager surfaces.
entries.remediation: Design a secure sandbox and implement the faithful coding-agent facade as explicit projects; do not emulate isolation with an unsafe stub.
entries.conditions: Load and execute a saved workflow with isolation assertions.

entries.explanation: The permission gate deliberately falls back to conservative whole-string parsing when tree-sitter WASM is unavailable, but the interactive config modal requires missing settings TUI components.
entries.remediation: Validate security decisions now; implement the settings components only if modal parity is required.
entries.conditions: Test allow, ask, and deny decisions including the parser fallback, then classify the config modal separately.

entries.explanation: The extension is a narrow command-only surface and the inspected entrypoint has no native, stream, socket, WASM, or private-SDK dependency.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Invoke the simplify command in a real session.

entries.explanation: Registration succeeds, but the main finder tools dynamically load @ff-labs/fff-node and invoke FileFinder, so useful work reaches a native backend Sobek cannot execute.
entries.remediation: Provide an upstream-supported executable or service backend, or undertake native-addon bridging separately.
entries.conditions: Execute finder, read, and edit tools rather than checking registration alone.

entries.explanation: The initial prototype failure comes from ws extending an absent stream class, while task networking and configuration also require net, TLS, SettingsManager, DefaultPackageManager, and SettingsList.
entries.remediation: Treat real streams and sockets plus the private settings/TUI surface as a broad compatibility project.
entries.conditions: Load all entrypoints and test websocket, task orchestration, and config UI paths.

entries.explanation: The declared entrypoint uses top-level await, which esbuild cannot emit into Pigo's synchronous CommonJS execution model.
entries.remediation: Design true ESM and asynchronous module execution or a proven semantics-preserving transform; changing only the JavaScript target is insufficient.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and open the question UI after module support exists.

entries.explanation: This is intentionally an event-only extension with no commands or tools and it deliberately disables itself without a key or local daemon; no unsupported static API was found.
entries.remediation: None in Pigo.
entries.conditions: Smoke credentialed event delivery and flush behavior, and verify deliberate no-key disablement.

entries.explanation: Memory persistence initializes a mandatory better-sqlite3 backend at load; the alternative in the package targets Bun rather than Sobek.
entries.remediation: Adopt an extension-supported non-native backend or design an explicit Go or service-backed store.
entries.conditions: Load, migrate the database, and store and query memory.

entries.explanation: The declared entrypoint and i18n bridge use top-level await, which Pigo's synchronous CommonJS execution model cannot compile.
entries.remediation: Design true ESM and asynchronous module execution or a semantics-preserving transform, then reassess UI compatibility.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and execute its todo UI and workflow.

entries.explanation: The previously missing isToolCallEventType helper is now present, and the inspected registration and tool paths expose no remaining native, socket, WASM, or private unresolved surface.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Smoke tool-call interception and each registered Hypa tool.

entries.explanation: The previously missing isContextOverflow helper is now present, and no remaining unsupported API was found in goal, lifecycle, or compaction paths.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Smoke goal completion and blocking plus compaction and session lifecycle.

entries.explanation: Commands register, but their checkpoint and rewind selectors construct Input, Text, and TruncatedText and call SessionManager.list or listAll, which Pigo does not expose.
entries.remediation: Implement faithful session-list APIs and the required TUI components as an explicit compatibility surface.
entries.conditions: Invoke checkpoint and rewind selectors over real sessions.

entries.explanation: Its two declared entrypoints fail independently: one dynamically loads @heyhuynhgiabuu/pi-pretty and the other eagerly requires the full native read, bash, edit, find, grep, ls, and write tool factories.
entries.remediation: Support executable package resolution and implement faithful native-tool factories.
entries.conditions: Load every manifest entrypoint and exercise all quiet tool replacements.

entries.explanation: The base tool delegates finite operations to a separate agent-browser executable on PATH, and those buffered-at-exit process calls fit current Pigo semantics.
entries.remediation: None for the base path.
entries.conditions: Install the matching agent-browser and smoke open, snapshot, and click; assess Electron or long-lived progress paths separately.

entries.explanation: Both tools use ordinary fetch and JSON against Ollama's local experimental search endpoints, with no unsupported Pigo API in the inspected paths.
entries.remediation: None in Pigo.
entries.conditions: Run both tools against a real Ollama endpoint that exposes the expected APIs.

entries.explanation: Finite read and view operations may consume buffered output, but edit, write, check, map, and identify write to child stdin, while grep requires a missing createGrepTool factory.
entries.remediation: Implement finite writable child stdin and the faithful grep factory; do not claim streaming subprocess parity from buffered execution.
entries.conditions: Test every registered tool, especially edit, write, check, map, identify, and grep.

entries.explanation: Dynamic registration waits for credentials at session_start, while the search path uses AbortSignal timeout and any plus streaming TextDecoder, all now implemented in Pigo.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: With a DeepSeek key, verify session_start registration, streaming search, cancellation, and timeout.

entries.explanation: Top-level await blocks compilation first, and the defining team workflows then require worker_threads and long-lived bidirectional child-process stdio.
entries.remediation: Treat ESM/TLA, workers, and process IPC as separate runtime design projects.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and test foreground and background team execution plus persistent state writes.

entries.explanation: After the IP-only net shim, loading reaches node:string_decoder, and the main sandbox still requires net.createServer plus a connected socket pair.
entries.remediation: Implement a real socket and stream runtime or provide an intentional native Go sandbox integration; a StringDecoder stub alone would be false compatibility.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and enforce a real sandboxed command and network decision.

entries.explanation: Loading first fails relative UMD requires, while main workflows also need ExtensionRunner interception, worker_threads, and net transport.
entries.remediation: Fix executable relative CommonJS resolution only as part of the broader runner, worker, and socket compatibility project.
entries.conditions: Load the package and test tool interception, regex memory, and Herdr transport.

entries.explanation: This event-only extension uses modelRegistry credential lookup and ordinary fetch, with no commands, tools, or unsupported static API.
entries.remediation: None in Pigo.
entries.conditions: Select a DeepSeek model and verify footer refresh and error handling with real credentials.

entries.explanation: The command, lifecycle, and model hooks use supported filesystem and path surfaces, and no native, stream, socket, WASM, or private unresolved API was found.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Invoke templates, model selection, and chained prompts.

entries.explanation: Registration succeeds with Pigo's IP-only net helpers, but the defining broker calls net.createServer and net.connect, which the runtime does not implement.
entries.remediation: Implement real socket semantics or provide an intentional native Go intercom transport.
entries.conditions: Start a broker and exchange messages between two live sessions.

entries.explanation: Registration succeeds, but loading the platform .node binding falls back to a throwing mock, and every useful indexing operation reaches that unavailable native implementation.
entries.remediation: Use an upstream-supported pure, service, or Go backend, or undertake native-addon support separately.
entries.conditions: Build an index and execute parse, search, and reference tools.

entries.explanation: The inspected Jira and Confluence paths use supported authentication storage, fetch, text, and arrayBuffer surfaces; only real credentials and network access are expected.
entries.remediation: None in Pigo.
entries.conditions: Run credentialed Jira and Confluence read and write smokes.

entries.explanation: Basic JSON tracing may be viable, but load-only evidence does not initialize or flush a trace and bundled media, attachment, stream, and hash paths reach Blob, FormData, ReadableStream, and crypto.subtle APIs.
entries.remediation: Run a credentialed plain-span smoke first, then add standards-faithful Web APIs only for paths proven reachable.
entries.conditions: Test plain spans separately from attachments, media, and streaming instrumentation.

entries.explanation: Bundled MCP code requires node:stream, while core initialization eagerly calls missing bash, read, find, grep, and ls tool-definition factories.
entries.remediation: Implement faithful native-tool-definition factories and a real MCP stream transport; a stream stub only exposes the next failure.
entries.conditions: Load and execute ctx_shell, read, grep, and the MCP bridge.

entries.explanation: Registration succeeds, but LSP requires a persistent child process with writable stdin and streamed stdout, while Pigo currently buffers output until exit and exposes no writable pipe.
entries.remediation: Implement real os/exec pipe streaming, lifecycle, cancellation, and shutdown semantics.
entries.conditions: Initialize a real language server, send requests, receive diagnostics, and shut it down cleanly.

entries.explanation: The mandatory AST backend loads native tree-sitter and grammar addons; optional LSP code can degrade when vscode-jsonrpc is missing, but AST initialization cannot.
entries.remediation: Use an upstream-supported alternate parser backend or undertake native-addon support separately.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and generate a repository map and symbol graph.

entries.explanation: The Cursor SDK build resolves bun:sqlite and references declaration maps and package JavaScript artifacts that Pigo's bundler cannot load; ignoring the map artifacts would still leave the mandatory Bun backend.
entries.remediation: Use an upstream Node-compatible Cursor SDK build and exports or create a dedicated integration; do not stub bun:sqlite.
entries.conditions: Load the extension and run a real Cursor SDK agent.

entries.explanation: Core configuration eagerly constructs SettingsManager and calls readStoredCredential, so model registration cannot initialize without faithful private settings and credential APIs.
entries.remediation: Implement SettingsManager and readStoredCredential against Pigo's existing stores.
entries.conditions: Load, resolve global and project settings, authenticate, and register and select a model.

entries.explanation: The core memory pipeline depends on LanceDB .node bindings, onnxruntime-node binaries, and sharp native assets through @lancedb/lancedb and @xenova/transformers.
entries.remediation: Use extension-specific remote or Go backends, or undertake native runtime support as a separate design project.
entries.conditions: Load, embed, index, persist, and query a vault.

entries.explanation: A configured healthy Engram service is statically viable with the fixed AbortSignal timeout path, but cold auto-start expects truly detached child semantics that Pigo's tracked buffered execution does not provide.
entries.remediation: Require a pre-running ENGRAM_URL for now, or implement faithful detached child lifecycle as a broader process change.
entries.conditions: Test an already-running daemon separately from cold auto-start and shutdown.

entries.explanation: Loading independently hits missing WebAssembly and incorrect bundled import.meta asset URLs, while deeper read and replace paths require createReadTool and Markdown.
entries.remediation: Provide a hash backend or WASM support, preserve per-module asset URLs during bundling, then implement the required tool factory and TUI surface.
entries.conditions: Load the extension, read anchors, replace content, and render results.

entries.explanation: The inspected command and security hook paths use supported synchronous filesystem, path, crypto, and execFileSync APIs, with no native, socket, WASM, or private unresolved dependency.
entries.remediation: None statically.
entries.conditions: Behaviorally smoke allowed and blocked destructive commands plus redaction.
